About: It is about the final judgment of every person on earth.
It is about the pictural representation of the final judgment.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the late Cretan school.
It is associated with Georgios Klontzas.
It is associated with Leos Moskos.
ConsistsOf: It consists of egg tempera and gold leaf on wood.
It consists of Jesus, John the Baptist, apostles, and the Virgin Mary.
It consists of the Archangel Michael holding a sword and scale.
It consists of the prophet Daniel.
It consists of clergy, kings, nobles, and righteous women.
It consists of half-naked men waiting to be judged.
It consists of a group of people.
It consists of an angel carrying a scroll with names.
It consists of a red angel and the grim reaper.
It consists of a ship traveling in the water.
CreatedBy: It was created by Francheskos Kavertzas.
HasA: It has a nun named Evgenia Trapezontiopoulla.
It has Jesus on the vertical axis at the top.
It has John the Baptist to Jesus' left.
It has the Virgin Mary to Jesus' right.
It has a band introducing the Second Coming.
It has the book of life open.
It has the Archangel Michael holding a sword and scale.
It has the prophet Daniel on a cloud.
It has three rows featuring clergy, kings, nobles, and righteous women.
It has a group of half-naked men waiting to be judged.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the presence of a nun.
It is characterized by the weighing of souls.
It is characterized by a depiction of hell with dragons, demons, and snakes.
It is characterized by paradise with Christ as High-Priest.
It is characterized by the Gates of Paradise.
HeldIn: It was held in the collection of the Hellenic Institute of Venice in Italy.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Georgios Klontzas's The Last Judgment.
It was influenced by earlier artists' prototypes.
IsA: It is an egg tempera painting.
It is a pictural representation of the final judgment.
It is a work of art.
KnownAs: It is known as The Second Coming.
KnownFor: It is known for featuring a nun named Evgenia Trapezontiopoulla.
It is known for its depiction of hell.
It is known for its representation of the final judgment.
MemberOf: It is a member of the late Cretan school.
PartOf: It is part of the collection of the Hellenic Institute of Venice in Italy.
